revision_summary: One critical fabrication requires correction and cascading fixes: Stage 3 misattributes the line 'Jeez. That's a lot. Okay.' at 05:52 to the rep (speaker_0) when it is spoken by James (speaker_1). This misattribution corrupts the coaching narrative in cherry_pick_moments[1], multi_touch_patterns[0].stewart_read, and the coaching_focus[1].why entry. All three sections must be revised to reflect that the rep's actual response to the 1,000 kWh disclosure was 'Mhmm' — a minimal acknowledgment — which actually strengthens the bill-anchor-flip coaching gap (no reaction at all, not a reaction without follow-through). The coaching question 'You said jeez, that's a lot — you felt it. Did James feel it?' must be rewritten accordingly. Additionally: (1) cherry_pick_moments[0] (pool at 03:54) must acknowledge that the rep's deferral to the specialist is consistent with setter_scope_discipline and the alternative_hypothesis is better supported by the codex than the reactive read — revise to present both readings with appropriate weight; (2) rep_intent.evidence[2] must be corrected — the rep DID attempt to capture the referral name at 06:31 ('Do you know her name?'), so the claim of 'no attempt to capture' is false; (3) cherry_pick_moments[4] (referral) classification should be changed from 'rapport_repair' to 'trust_signal_handling' and a codex reference (analysis_directives.trust_building_micro_trades) should be added; (4) button_up assessment in cherry_pick_moments[5] should be corrected to note that appointment_datetime_confirmed, phone_appointment_specified, and text_followup_promise were completed — 'skipped almost entirely' overstates the miss; (5) the objection_inversion codex reference in multi_touch_patterns[0] should be corrected to coaching_philosophy.bill_anchoring since James raised no objection — he was volunteering enthusiasm signals; (6) add missed cherry_pick moment for the $7 bill / utility switch handling at 03:16 as a positive rep instinct worth reinforcing.
